title: schoenus confervoides
intro: Schoenus confervoides, also known as the Cumbungi or Saw-sedge, is a perennial aquatic plant native to Australia. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Aboriginal medicine, and its medicinal properties include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Schoenus confervoides are said to include relief from fever, pain, and inflammation, as well as its use as an antiseptic for wound treatment. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used in infusions, decoctions, or as a poultice to harness its medicinal properties.
